guides.title: Setting Up 'Hey Siri'
guides.description: Learn how to activate Siri and train it to recognize your unique voice.
steps: Open the 'Settings' app on your iPhone.
Scroll down and tap on 'Siri & Search'.
Toggle the switch next to 'Listen for Hey Siri' so it turns green.
Follow the on-screen prompts, reading the sentences aloud to train Siri to your voice.

guides.title: Asking for Weather and Time
guides.description: Use Siri to quickly check the local weather forecast or the time anywhere in the world.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri' to activate the assistant.
Ask 'What is the weather like today?' for a local forecast.
Ask 'Will it rain tomorrow?' for future weather predictions.
Ask 'What time is it in London?' to check global time zones.

guides.title: Making Hands-Free Calls
guides.description: Call your friends, family, or local businesses without touching your screen.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, call [Contact Name]'.
If the contact has multiple numbers, Siri will ask which one to call (e.g., Mobile or Work).
You can also say 'Hey Siri, call the nearest pizza place' to search and call local businesses.
To end a call hands-free, simply say 'Hey Siri, hang up'.

guides.title: Sending Text Messages
guides.description: Dictate and send text messages through iMessage or standard SMS.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, send a message to [Contact Name]'.
Siri will ask what you want to say. Speak your message clearly.
Siri will read the message back to you for confirmation.
Say 'Yes' or 'Send' to deliver the message.

guides.title: Setting Alarms and Timers
guides.description: Perfect for cooking or waking up, let Siri manage your countdowns and alarms.
steps: To set a timer, say 'Hey Siri, set a timer for 15 minutes'.
To check a timer, say 'Hey Siri, how much time is left?'.
To set a morning alarm, say 'Hey Siri, wake me up at 6 AM'.
To turn off an active ringing alarm, just say 'Hey Siri, stop'.

guides.title: Creating Reminders
guides.description: Never forget a task by having Siri add items to your Reminders app.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, remind me to buy milk tomorrow at 5 PM'.
Siri will automatically create a time-based notification.
You can also use location-based reminders by saying 'Hey Siri, remind me to call Mom when I get home'.
View all your tasks by opening the 'Reminders' app.

guides.title: Managing Calendar Events
guides.description: Keep your schedule organized by adding and checking calendar appointments.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, schedule a meeting with John for Thursday at 2 PM'.
To check your schedule, ask 'Hey Siri, what is on my calendar for today?'.
If you need to move an event, say 'Hey Siri, reschedule my 2 PM meeting to 4 PM'.
Siri will sync these changes directly to your Apple Calendar.

guides.title: Playing Music and Audio
guides.description: Control Apple Music, Spotify, and podcasts using just your voice.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, play some relaxing music'.
To skip a track, say 'Hey Siri, next song'.
To control volume, say 'Hey Siri, turn the volume up' or 'turn the volume to 50 percent'.
You can also ask 'Hey Siri, what song is playing right now?' to identify music.

guides.title: Getting Navigation Directions
guides.description: Get turn-by-turn directions and check traffic conditions for your commute.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, give me directions to the nearest gas station'.
Siri will open Apple Maps and begin routing.
Ask 'Hey Siri, what is my ETA?' to know when you will arrive.
Ask 'Hey Siri, is there traffic on my way home?' to check for delays.

guides.title: Math and Unit Conversions
guides.description: Solve quick math problems, split bills, and convert currencies instantly.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, what is 45 multiplied by 12?'.
For splitting a bill, ask 'Hey Siri, what is a 20 percent tip on 85 dollars?'.
For currency, ask 'Hey Siri, how much is 50 US Dollars in Indonesian Rupiah?'.
For measurements, ask 'Hey Siri, how many kilometers are in 10 miles?'.

guides.title: Translating Languages
guides.description: Use Siri as a pocket translator when traveling or learning new languages.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, how do you say where is the train station in Japanese?'.
Siri will speak the translation out loud and show it on the screen.
You can ask for translations in dozens of languages, including Spanish, French, and Mandarin.
Tap the play button on the screen to hear the pronunciation again.

guides.title: Finding Misplaced Devices
guides.description: Use Siri to ping your Apple Watch, AirPods, or iPad if you lose them nearby.
steps: Say 'Hey Siri, find my Apple Watch' or 'Where are my AirPods?'.
Siri will connect to the Find My network.
If the device is nearby and connected, Siri will make it play a loud ringing sound.
Follow the sound to locate your missing device.

guides.title: Controlling Smart Home Devices
guides.description: Control lights, locks, and thermostats connected to your Apple Home app.
steps: Ensure your smart devices are linked to the 'Home' app on your iPhone.
Say 'Hey Siri, turn off the living room lights'.
Say 'Hey Siri, set the thermostat to 22 degrees'.
Say 'Hey Siri, lock the front door' for enhanced home security.

guides.title: Using Custom Siri Shortcuts
guides.description: (Advanced) Create powerful, multi-step automations triggered by a single phrase.
steps: Open the 'Shortcuts' app on your iPhone.
Tap the '+' icon to create a new shortcut and add actions (e.g., Get Directions to Home AND Play Driving Playlist).
Tap the name at the top to rename it to a phrase you want to say, like 'Heading Home'.
Now, simply say 'Hey Siri, Heading Home' and Siri will execute all steps automatically.

guides.title: Privacy and Disabling 'Hey Siri'
guides.description: Manage your privacy settings and learn how to turn off voice activation.
steps: Open the 'Settings' app and tap on 'Siri & Search'.
To turn off voice activation, toggle off 'Listen for Hey Siri'.
To delete your audio history, tap 'Siri & Dictation History' and select 'Delete'.
If you only want to use Siri by holding the side power button, ensure 'Press Side Button for Siri' remains toggled on.
